ab: nu aremameremeng averuk lra i wadilri na ma’inay.
ch: 今天傍晚，弟弟就要回家了。
en: My younger brother is going home this evening.

ab: salrangan ku kani mulri na vavayan mukuwa i iciba tremima’a, padulu mi za keraravien kani wadilri na ma’inay.
ch: 我和奶奶去市場買雞，準備煮好吃的晚餐給弟弟。
en: My grandma and I went to the market to buy chickens and prepared a delicious dinner for my younger brother.

ab: lemavat mi kana menaniwan kana kulang, sazu na ma’ama’at maw ziya kiyakulang.
ch: 我們經過了菜攤，菜攤有好多新鮮的青菜。
en: We passed the vegetable stand, which had a lot of fresh green vegetables.

ab: tremima’ mi za takungkung, tavurayray zi maw na ninzing.
ch: 我們買了空心菜、山藥和胡蘿蔔。
en: We bought water spinach, yams and carrots.

ab: izu na i ngidiran na menaniwan kana inaekan na vuwa’ izu na tawki marengay, “tavu’ naku pangudangudalr zi maw naku lupas. alra trima’ za papidaya!”
ch: 旁邊水果攤的老闆說:「我的鳳梨和桃子很甜。買一些吧!」
en: The owner of the nearby fruit stand said: “My pineapples and peaches are very sweet. Buy some!”

ab: tremima’ mi za pangudangudalr zi lupas.
makalrang mi kana maniwan kana ’alum mu, izu na tawki mu marengay, “tratrima’ mu za eman? harem na vuyu ma’izang zi semever, saya mu telu leman paisu.”
ch: 我們經過了肉攤，老闆說:「你要買什麼?今天的雞又大又肥，一隻300元。」
en: We passed the meat stall, and the boss said, “What do you want to buy? Today's chickens are big and fat, and one is 300 NTD.”

ab: i mulri na vavayan mu pazangal zazu kema, kiumalr kana tawki aruwa u kadadulu kanatu trima’.
ch: 奶奶覺得太貴了，問老闆可不可以便宜一點。
en: Grandma thought it was too expensive and asked the boss if it could be cheaper.

ab: maw mu na tawki marengay madulu lra inia, ’azi ta lra kararuwa lemehetr.
ch: 但老闆說已經很便宜，不能再減了。
en: But the boss said that it is already very cheap and cannot be reduced any further.

ab: izu natu ’udar na tawki mu marengay, alamu trima’ kanaku vulraw, naku vulraw ma’izang zi madulu.
ch: 隔壁的老闆說，來買我的魚，我的魚又大又便宜。
en: The boss next door said, come and buy my fish, my fish is big and cheap.

ab: likuzan lra mu tremima’ mi za vuyu zi tremima’ mi za vulraw, muruma’ mi zi temusasiyaw mi lra za saya to a siyaw.
ch: 最後我們買了雞也買了魚，回家煮了一桌子的菜。
en: In the end we bought chicken and fish, and went home to cook a table of food.

ab: sawariyan mi mengara tivus ’aremeng lra na wari.
en: After waiting for a long time, it was already dark.

ab: pudingwa muruma’ i wadilri na ma’inay marengay mu, na hitay munamun lra ’azi talaaulep kanini na talaulepan kema zi, nu masalr lra kana katalaulepan mu aruwa ku lra muruma’.
ch: 弟弟打電話回來說，軍隊突然不能放假了，要下一個禮拜日才回來。
en: The younger brother called back and said that the army suddenly couldn't take a vacation and would not be back until the next Sunday.

ab: i mulri na vavayan marengay temusasiyaw ta za sakuwani sazu zi, ta rengayaw papiya salrangana ta mekan!
ch: 奶奶說已經煮了這麼多，叫大家一起來吃吧!
en: Grandma said that she has cooked so much, so let's eat together!

ab: mi dawlraw mi wadadiyan zi ali’an, salrangalrangan mi keravi semiyaw kanini na sazu na tinusasiyaw.
ch: 我們邀請了親朋好友，一起來享用豐盛的晚餐。
en: We invited friends and family to have a great dinner together.
